name: science_self_efficacy
description: Measures the respondent's perceived ability to understand, engage with, and assess their own knowledge of science and technology, capturing subjective competence in scientific and technological domains.

name: institutional_trust_in_science
description: Captures the degree to which respondents trust scientific institutions, researchers, and universities to act in the public interest and produce socially relevant knowledge, reflecting broader orientations toward epistemic authority.

name: science_technology_engagement
description: Measures the extent to which respondents actively seek out, consume, and engage with science and technology information across multiple media and informal channels outside of formal schooling.

name: information_capital
description: Assesses the household and personal stock of informational resources — including books of various types, computer ownership, and internet access — that enable access to knowledge and reflect socioeconomic and cultural capital.

name: science_technology_policy_attitudes
description: Captures respondents' normative beliefs about the role of science and technology in addressing Mexico's national problems, including views on domestic technology development, public financing, and the societal obligations of science.
